mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Include PKCE - Code Challenge in Proxy (2nd try)
mission.description: Include PKCE - Code Challenge in Proxy (2nd try). Requested by 20+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
## Expected Behavior

For security reasons It would be nice if the PKCE extension would be included in the proxy.
https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/pkce/.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Include PKCE - Code Challenge in Proxy (2nd try)
steps.description:
Adds Code Challenge PKCE support (RFC-7636) and partial
Authorization Server Metadata (RFC-8414) for detecting PKCE support.

- Introduces new option `--force-code-challenge-method` to force a
specific code challenge method (either `S256` or `plain`) for instances
when the server has not
```yaml
https://YOUR_DOMAIN/authorize?
    response_type=code&
    code_challenge=CODE_CHALLENGE&
    code_challenge_method=S256&
    client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&
    redirect_uri=YOUR_CALLBACK_URL&
    scope=SCOPE&
    state=STATE
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get events -n oauth2-proxy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Include PKCE - Code Challenge in Proxy (2nd try)" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adds Code Challenge PKCE support (RFC-7636) and partial
Authorization Server Metadata (RFC-8414) for detecting PKCE support.

- Introduces new option `--force-code-challenge-method` to force a
specific code challenge method (either `S256` or `plain`) for instances
when the server has not implemented RFC-8414 in order to detect
PKCE support on the discovery document.
codeSnippets:
https://YOUR_DOMAIN/authorize?
    response_type=code&
    code_challenge=CODE_CHALLENGE&
    code_challenge_method=S256&
    client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&
    redirect_uri=YOUR_CALLBACK_URL&
    scope=SCOPE&
    state=STATE

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
